summary: A Go monolith implementing an MCP-based runner with schema validation, currently in a post-release phase with significant AI-agent-generated test expansion.
concerns: high volume of AI-generated test iterations (25+ agent directories) suggests unstable test baseline
test file sprawl with multiple _extra_test.go variants indicates flaky or incomplete original test coverage
potential redundancy between agent-generated tests and main test files
clean git status post-release suggests tests may have been force-committed without proper integration
suggestions: consolidate extra test files into main test suites to reduce maintenance burden
review and deduplicate the 25+ agent-generated test directories
establish stricter test stability gates before merging agent-generated code
focus on core runner and MCP handler reliability rather than expanding test permutations
